name: Choline sensor (BetI + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects choline, a dietary precursor of the cardiovascular-risk metabolite TMA/TMAO in the gut. The Marionette BetI sensor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917.

input.detects: Luminal choline, the dietary precursor of gut-microbial TMA (and host TMAO)

sensing.sensorMechanism: BetI de-represses the Pbet promoter on binding choline.
sensing.signalFlow: Choline -> BetI releases Pbet -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT). Pair an inverter for turn-on monitoring of dietary choline load.

parts.name: BetI regulator
parts.source: Marionette sensor (Meyer et al. 2019); E. coli bet system

parts.name: sfGFP reporter
parts.notes: Recoverable from stool for non-invasive readout.

performance.notes: BetI is a characterised orthogonal Marionette sensor; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template. Relevant to TMAO/cardiovascular research.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the reporter promoter; check Nissle off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: BetI + Pbet -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + constitutive sfGFP. Low-copy vector.

buildSteps.title: Validate in vitro
buildSteps.description: Confirm choline response across a standard curve before any animal work.

buildSteps.title: Recover and measure
buildSteps.description: Recover cells from stool; quantify fluorescence.

provenance.source: Design template combining the Marionette BetI choline sensor with a dCas9 CRISPRi circuit in E. coli Nissle
partsSources: Meyer et al. 2019, Nat. Chem. Biol. (Marionette sensors, BetI)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
